sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: EC2 Instance Store vs Amazon EBS
sections.contentHtml:
EC2 Instance StoreTemporary/ephemeral block storage physically attached to the host server. Low-latency access. Data is permanently deleted when the EC2 instance is stopped or terminated. Ideal for buffers, caches, scratch data.
Amazon EBSPersistent block storage. Data survives stop/restart/terminate. Attached over network. Zonal — must be in the same AZ as the EC2 instance. Encrypted via AWS KMS.

EBS Volume Types

General Purpose SSDBalanced IOPS and cost — most common
Provisioned IOPS SSDHigh IOPS — databases, I/O-intensive workloads
Throughput Optimized HDDHigh throughput (MB/s) — big data, streaming
Cold HDDLowest cost — infrequently accessed cold data
EBS Multi-Attach: Attach one Provisioned IOPS volume to multiple EC2 instances — BUT instances CANNOT concurrently modify the same file. Use EFS if you need concurrent writes.
sections.contentVi: EC2 Instance Store là lưu trữ block tạm thời gắn trực tiếp vào host server — truy cập nhanh, độ trễ thấp. Dữ liệu bị xóa vĩnh viễn khi EC2 instance bị dừng hoặc terminate. Phù hợp cho buffer, cache, scratch data. Amazon EBS là lưu trữ block persistent — dữ liệu tồn tại sau khi stop/restart/terminate. Kết nối qua mạng, là zonal (phải cùng AZ với EC2). Mã hóa qua AWS KMS. Bốn loại EBS: General Purpose SSD (cân bằng IOPS và chi phí, phổ biến nhất), Provisioned IOPS SSD (IOPS cao cho database và workload I/O intensive), Throughput Optimized HDD (throughput MB/s cao cho big data, streaming), Cold HDD (rẻ nhất cho dữ liệu truy cập không thường xuyên). EBS Multi-Attach: gắn một volume Provisioned IOPS vào nhiều EC2 nhưng KHÔNG thể ghi đồng thời cùng file — dùng EFS nếu cần concurrent writes.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service)
sections.contentHtml:
S3 is an object storage service with virtually unlimited capacity. Files (objects) are stored in buckets and accessed via REST API.

Storage Classes

S3 StandardFrequently accessed data
S3 Intelligent-TieringChanging or unknown access patterns
S3 Standard-IALong-lived, infrequently accessed
S3 One Zone-IAInfrequent access, single AZ (lower cost)
S3 GlacierArchival — min 90 days
S3 Glacier Deep Archive7–10 year archival — min 180 days, cheapest

Key Features

  • Lifecycle Policy — auto-transition between storage classes
  • Cross-Region Replication (CRR) — replicate to another region
  • Versioning + MFA Delete — prevent accidental deletion
  • S3 Transfer Acceleration + Multipart Upload — faster uploads
sections.contentVi: S3 là dịch vụ object storage với dung lượng gần như không giới hạn. Files (objects) lưu trong buckets và truy cập qua REST API. Sáu storage class: S3 Standard (dữ liệu truy cập thường xuyên), S3 Intelligent-Tiering (access pattern thay đổi hoặc chưa biết), S3 Standard-IA (lâu dài, truy cập không thường xuyên), S3 One Zone-IA (không thường xuyên, một AZ, rẻ hơn), S3 Glacier (archive tối thiểu 90 ngày), S3 Glacier Deep Archive (archive 7-10 năm, tối thiểu 180 ngày, rẻ nhất). Tính năng quan trọng: Lifecycle Policy — tự động chuyển giữa các storage class; Cross-Region Replication (CRR) — sao chép sang region khác; Versioning + MFA Delete — chống xóa nhầm; S3 Transfer Acceleration + Multipart Upload — upload nhanh hơn.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: S3 Glacier — Archival Storage
sections.contentHtml:
S3 Glacier stores cold (rarely accessed) data at very low cost. Files are called vaults.

Minimum storage durations (exam trap):
• S3 Glacier: 90 days — delete before 90 days = charged for the full 90 days
• S3 Glacier Deep Archive: 180 days — cheapest for 7–10 year archives

Retrieval Options

Expedited1–5 minutes (highest cost)
Standard3–5 hours
Bulk5–12 hours (lowest cost)
If you store temporary data (hours/days), use S3 Standard — Glacier's minimum duration charges make it more expensive for short-lived data.
sections.contentVi: S3 Glacier lưu trữ dữ liệu cold (hiếm khi truy cập) với chi phí rất thấp. Files được gọi là vaults. Thời gian lưu trữ tối thiểu là bẫy thi hay gặp: S3 Glacier tối thiểu 90 ngày — xóa trước 90 ngày vẫn bị tính phí cho đủ 90 ngày. S3 Glacier Deep Archive tối thiểu 180 ngày — rẻ nhất cho archive 7-10 năm. Ba lựa chọn retrieval: Expedited (1-5 phút, chi phí cao nhất), Standard (3-5 giờ), Bulk (5-12 giờ, rẻ nhất). Lưu ý: nếu dữ liệu chỉ lưu vài giờ/ngày, hãy dùng S3 Standard — thời gian tối thiểu của Glacier khiến nó đắt hơn cho dữ liệu ngắn hạn.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: EFS & FSx — Shared File Systems
sections.contentHtml:
Amazon EFSShared Linux file system. POSIX-compliant, uses NFS protocol. Mountable to multiple EC2 instances across different AZs. Supports concurrent writes. Standard and Infrequent Access classes.
FSx for LustreHigh-performance parallel file system for Linux only. Used for HPC, ML. Hundreds of GB/s throughput and millions of IOPS. Mounts to EC2 or EKS containers via CSI.
FSx for Windows ServerFully managed Windows file server. Uses SMB protocol. Integrates with Active Directory. For SharePoint, SQL Server, Windows containers.
EFS and FSx for Lustre = Linux only. FSx for Windows = Windows only. Need concurrent writes across multiple EC2? → EFS (not EBS Multi-Attach).
sections.contentVi: Amazon EFS là hệ thống file Linux dùng chung, tuân thủ POSIX, dùng giao thức NFS. Có thể mount cho nhiều EC2 ở các AZ khác nhau. Hỗ trợ concurrent writes. Có hai storage class: Standard và Infrequent Access. Amazon FSx for Lustre là hệ thống file song song hiệu năng cao chỉ dành cho Linux — dùng cho HPC và ML. Throughput hàng trăm GB/s và hàng triệu IOPS. Mount vào EC2 hoặc EKS containers qua CSI. Amazon FSx for Windows Server là file server Windows được quản lý hoàn toàn, dùng giao thức SMB, tích hợp với Active Directory. Dành cho SharePoint, SQL Server, Windows containers. Tóm tắt: EFS và FSx Lustre = chỉ Linux. FSx Windows = chỉ Windows. Cần concurrent writes nhiều EC2 → EFS, không phải EBS Multi-Attach.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: AWS Backup & Storage Gateway
sections.contentHtml:
AWS BackupCentralized backup service. Automates server and DB backups. RDS default max retention = 35 days; AWS Backup allows 90+ days or years. Lifecycle policy to move backups to cold storage.

AWS Storage Gateway (Hybrid)

File GatewayStore/retrieve objects in S3 via NFS or SMB from on-premises
Volume GatewayBlock storage via iSCSI. Cache mode (data in S3, hot cache local) or Stored mode (full dataset on-prem, async backup to S3)
Tape GatewayVirtual tape library (VTL) — replace physical tapes. Archives to S3 Glacier or Deep Archive.
Storage Gateway vs DataSync: Gateway = active hybrid (on-prem sends data to AWS). DataSync = full migration (decommission on-prem storage).
sections.contentVi: AWS Backup là dịch vụ backup tập trung — tự động hóa backup cho server và database. RDS mặc định chỉ giữ tối đa 35 ngày; AWS Backup cho phép giữ 90 ngày đến nhiều năm. Có lifecycle policy để chuyển backup sang cold storage. AWS Storage Gateway (Hybrid) có 3 loại: File Gateway — lưu trữ và truy xuất objects trong S3 qua NFS hoặc SMB từ on-premises; Volume Gateway — block storage qua iSCSI với Cache mode (dữ liệu trong S3, hot cache lưu tại local) hoặc Stored mode (toàn bộ dataset on-premises, async backup lên S3); Tape Gateway — virtual tape library (VTL) thay thế băng vật lý, archive lên S3 Glacier hoặc Deep Archive. Phân biệt quan trọng: Storage Gateway = hybrid đang hoạt động (on-premises tiếp tục gửi dữ liệu lên AWS). DataSync = di chuyển toàn bộ để loại bỏ on-premises storage.

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Storage Decision Guide
sections.contentHtml:
• Temporary EC2 storage, lost on stop? → Instance Store
  • Persistent block storage for 1 EC2? → EBS
  • Shared block storage for multiple EC2 (no concurrent writes)? → EBS Multi-Attach (PIOPS only)
  • Shared file system, concurrent writes, Linux? → EFS
  • Shared file system, Windows? → FSx for Windows
  • Object storage, unlimited? → S3
  • Long-term archive >90 days? → S3 Glacier
  • 7–10 year archive, cheapest? → S3 Glacier Deep Archive
  • Hybrid: connect on-prem to cloud? → Storage Gateway
  • Full migration to cloud? → DataSync
sections.contentVi: Bảng quyết định lưu trữ nhanh: EC2 tạm thời mất khi dừng → Instance Store. Block storage persistent cho 1 EC2 → EBS. Shared block storage nhiều EC2 (không ghi đồng thời) → EBS Multi-Attach (chỉ PIOPS). Hệ thống file chia sẻ, ghi đồng thời, Linux → EFS. Hệ thống file Windows → FSx for Windows. Object storage không giới hạn → S3. Archive dài hạn >90 ngày → S3 Glacier. Archive 7-10 năm, rẻ nhất → S3 Glacier Deep Archive. Hybrid kết nối on-premises với cloud → Storage Gateway. Di chuyển toàn bộ lên cloud → DataSync.

summary: Instance Store = ephemeral (lost on stop/terminate); EBS = persistent, zonal, SSD (IOPS) or HDD (throughput), 4 types
EBS Multi-Attach: Provisioned IOPS only, no concurrent file writes — use EFS for concurrent writes
S3 Glacier minimum 90 days; Deep Archive minimum 180 days — early deletion still billed for full period
EFS = shared Linux NFS (multi-AZ concurrent); FSx for Lustre = HPC Linux; FSx for Windows = SMB + Active Directory
Storage Gateway (hybrid active) vs DataSync (full migration) — critical distinction for hybrid scenarios
summaryVi: Instance Store = tạm thời (mất khi stop/terminate); EBS = persistent, zonal, SSD (IOPS) hoặc HDD (throughput), 4 loại
EBS Multi-Attach: chỉ Provisioned IOPS, không ghi đồng thời cùng file — dùng EFS cho concurrent writes
S3 Glacier tối thiểu 90 ngày; Deep Archive tối thiểu 180 ngày — xóa sớm vẫn tính phí cho cả kỳ tối thiểu
EFS = Linux NFS chia sẻ (nhiều AZ, concurrent); FSx for Lustre = HPC Linux; FSx for Windows = SMB + Active Directory
Storage Gateway (hybrid đang hoạt động) so với DataSync (di chuyển toàn bộ) — phân biệt quan trọng cho tình huống hybrid

quiz.question: A company runs a high-performance computing (HPC) workload on Linux EC2 instances that requires shared storage with sub-millisecond latency and hundreds of gigabytes per second of throughput. Which storage solution is MOST appropriate?
options: Amazon EFS with General Purpose performance mode
Amazon FSx for Lustre
Amazon EBS io2 with Multi-Attach enabled
Amazon FSx for Windows File Server
quiz.explanation: FSx for Lustre is purpose-built for HPC Linux workloads requiring extreme throughput and low latency. EFS General Purpose is suitable for general shared file storage but not HPC-level performance. EBS Multi-Attach is block storage limited to a single AZ and does not support concurrent file system writes. FSx for Windows is designed for Windows workloads using SMB.

quiz.question: A startup stores frequently accessed application data on an EC2 instance store. An engineer warns that stopping or terminating the instance will cause data loss. The team wants PERSISTENT storage with minimal changes to the application. What should they use?
options: Attach an Amazon EBS volume to the instance
Enable EC2 hibernation to preserve instance store data
Copy data to another EC2 instance's instance store
Enable EBS encryption on the existing instance store
quiz.explanation: Amazon EBS provides persistent block storage that survives instance stop and termination, making it the correct replacement for ephemeral instance store volumes. EC2 hibernation does not persist instance store data across termination. Instance store data cannot be copied to another instance store for persistence. Instance store volumes cannot be encrypted as EBS — they are physically attached and not EBS.

quiz.question: A financial application requires a database volume that sustains 64,000 IOPS with consistent sub-millisecond latency. The database must also be accessible from multiple EC2 instances simultaneously in the same Availability Zone. Which EBS configuration supports this?
options: gp3 volume with Multi-Attach enabled
st1 (Throughput Optimized HDD) with Multi-Attach enabled
io2 Block Express with Multi-Attach enabled
gp2 volume striped across multiple instances
quiz.explanation: EBS Multi-Attach is exclusively supported on Provisioned IOPS (io1/io2) volumes, not on gp2, gp3, or HDD types. io2 Block Express supports up to 256,000 IOPS and is the only valid Multi-Attach option here. Note that Multi-Attach does NOT support concurrent file system writes — the application must manage concurrent access at the cluster level.

quiz.question: A media company needs to store large video files (averaging 500 GB each) and requires throughput of 500 MB/s for sequential read/write operations during batch processing. Cost optimization is a priority. Which EBS volume type is MOST cost-effective?
options: gp3 (General Purpose SSD)
io2 (Provisioned IOPS SSD)
st1 (Throughput Optimized HDD)
sc1 (Cold HDD)
quiz.explanation: st1 (Throughput Optimized HDD) is designed for large sequential workloads like big data, log processing, and video processing, delivering up to 500 MB/s throughput at a much lower cost than SSD options. sc1 is even cheaper but capped at 250 MB/s. io2 is for high IOPS transactional workloads and would be expensive for this use case. gp3 is a good general-purpose SSD but more expensive than HDD for pure throughput-heavy sequential I/O.

quiz.question: A company is migrating a Windows-based file server to AWS. The solution must support Active Directory authentication and allow multiple Windows EC2 instances to access shared files concurrently using SMB protocol. (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon EFS with Windows compatibility mode enabled
Amazon FSx for Windows File Server
Join FSx to the company's AWS Managed Microsoft AD or self-managed AD
Amazon FSx for Lustre with SMB mount targets
Amazon EBS io2 with Multi-Attach across multiple instances
quiz.explanation: FSx for Windows File Server natively supports SMB protocol and Active Directory integration, making it the correct service for Windows shared file storage. It must be joined to an Active Directory (AWS Managed or self-managed) for AD authentication. EFS is a Linux NFS service and does not support SMB or AD natively. FSx for Lustre is designed for Linux HPC workloads and does not support SMB. EBS Multi-Attach is block storage, not a shared file system.

quiz.question: A company archives compliance records in S3 Glacier. A record uploaded 60 days ago must be deleted due to a regulatory correction. The company is concerned about storage costs for the deletion. What will happen?
options: The object is deleted immediately with no additional charge
The company is billed for the remaining 30 days of the 90-day minimum storage period
The company is billed for a full additional 90 days starting from deletion
Deletion is blocked until the 90-day minimum period elapses
quiz.explanation: S3 Glacier has a minimum storage duration of 90 days. If an object is deleted before 90 days, AWS still charges for the remainder of the 90-day period (in this case, 30 days). The deletion itself is allowed — it is not blocked. You are not charged a full extra 90 days, only the prorated remainder. This early-deletion fee is a critical cost consideration for compliance archives.

quiz.question: A data archival solution stores objects in S3 Glacier Deep Archive. An audit requires retrieving an object that was stored 150 days ago, but the team now wants to delete it. What is the MINIMUM additional storage charge the company will incur for this early deletion?
options: No additional charge since 150 days have already been stored
Charge for 30 remaining days of the 180-day minimum period
Charge for a full additional 180 days
Charge for 90 days since Glacier Deep Archive inherits the Glacier minimum
quiz.explanation: S3 Glacier Deep Archive has a minimum storage duration of 180 days. At 150 days, the object has 30 days remaining in its minimum period. Deleting early triggers an early deletion fee for those 30 days. The charge is not for a full additional 180 days — only the prorated remainder. Deep Archive has its own 180-day minimum, not 90 days.

quiz.question: A company runs multiple Linux-based microservices on EC2 instances across three Availability Zones. Each service needs to read and write to the same shared configuration files simultaneously. Which storage solution provides the HIGHEST availability with LEAST operational overhead?
options: Amazon EBS gp3 volume replicated across AZs using a custom script
Amazon EFS with Multi-AZ mount targets
Amazon S3 with a custom FUSE driver mounted on each instance
Amazon FSx for Lustre in persistent mode
quiz.explanation: Amazon EFS is a managed NFS file system that supports concurrent access from multiple EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones, making it ideal for shared Linux file storage with high availability. EBS is zonal and cannot be natively shared across AZs. S3 with FUSE is possible but adds operational overhead and latency compared to EFS. FSx for Lustre is optimized for HPC throughput-intensive workloads, not general shared configuration files.

quiz.question: An on-premises application needs to access AWS S3 data as if it were a local NFS file share, while keeping the primary data in S3. The team wants to minimize changes to the on-premises application. Which solution has the LEAST operational overhead?
options: AWS DataSync with NFS as source and S3 as destination
AWS Storage Gateway File Gateway
AWS Direct Connect with Amazon EFS mount targets on-premises
AWS Snowball Edge as a persistent local cache
quiz.explanation: Storage Gateway File Gateway presents S3 storage as an NFS (or SMB) mount point to on-premises applications, requiring no application changes. Data is stored in S3 and cached locally. DataSync is designed for one-time or scheduled data migration/replication, not for ongoing active file access. Direct Connect with EFS is a valid architecture but more complex and expensive to operate. Snowball Edge is used for large-scale offline transfers, not ongoing active hybrid access.

quiz.question: A company is decommissioning its on-premises data center and must transfer 2 PB of archival data to AWS S3 Glacier as quickly as possible. After migration, no further on-premises access is needed. Which service is MOST appropriate?
options: AWS Storage Gateway Volume Gateway
AWS DataSync with direct connect
AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized devices
AWS Storage Gateway Tape Gateway
quiz.explanation: For a full, one-time migration of petabyte-scale data, AWS Snowball Edge devices are the most practical solution — physical devices are shipped, loaded offline, and returned to AWS for bulk import into S3 or Glacier. DataSync over Direct Connect can work for ongoing replication but transferring 2 PB over network is extremely slow. Storage Gateway is designed for hybrid active access, not large-scale migration. Tape Gateway replaces on-premises tape infrastructure with virtual tapes in Glacier but is not suited for a bulk migration scenario.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is designing storage for a new application. Two EC2 instances in the same Availability Zone must share a block storage volume and each instance may write to it. The application uses a cluster-aware file system to coordinate writes. Which statements about this architecture are correct? (Choose TWO)
options: This requires an EBS io1 or io2 volume with Multi-Attach enabled
EBS gp3 can be used with Multi-Attach for this scenario
Multi-Attach supports up to 16 instances in the same AZ
EFS is a better choice because it natively handles concurrent writes without application-level coordination
Multi-Attach volumes can be attached to instances across different Availability Zones
quiz.explanation: EBS Multi-Attach is only available on Provisioned IOPS (io1/io2) volumes, not gp2 or gp3. Multi-Attach supports up to 16 instances within the same Availability Zone. The question specifies a cluster-aware file system managing coordination, which is the required setup for Multi-Attach. Multi-Attach does NOT work across AZs — EBS is a zonal service. EFS is correct for concurrent writes without cluster coordination, but the question specifically asks about block storage with cluster-aware file system.

quiz.question: A web application generates log files that are accessed frequently during the first 30 days, rarely from day 31-90, and then must be retained for 7 years for compliance. The company wants the MOST cost-effective S3 storage lifecycle. After 7 years, objects can be deleted. Which storage class should objects transition to after 90 days?
options: S3 Standard-IA, then S3 Glacier after 90 days
S3 Glacier after 30 days, then S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 90 days
S3 Glacier after 90 days, then S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 180 days
S3 Glacier Deep Archive directly after 90 days
quiz.explanation: After 90 days of rare access and with a 7-year retention requirement, S3 Glacier Deep Archive is the MOST cost-effective option — it is the lowest-cost AWS storage class and is designed exactly for long-term compliance retention where retrieval latency (hours) is acceptable. Transitioning to Glacier first and then Deep Archive adds unnecessary cost and complexity. Transitioning to Glacier after just 30 days would trigger early access retrieval fees during the 31-90 day occasional access window.

quiz.question: A gaming company runs a leaderboard service on an EC2 instance using a local NVMe instance store for caching. After a planned maintenance stop-start cycle, the leaderboard data is gone. The team wants the cache to survive stop-start cycles without switching to a different service. What should they do?
options: Enable instance store snapshot backups before stopping
Migrate the cache to an EBS gp3 volume
Use EC2 Instance Connect to copy data before stopping
Convert the instance store to an EBS-backed volume using AWS CLI
quiz.explanation: Instance store volumes are ephemeral — data is permanently lost on any stop or termination event. The correct fix is to migrate the cache to an EBS volume, which persists across stop-start cycles. Instance store volumes cannot be snapshotted, converted to EBS, or made persistent by any configuration change. Manually copying data before each stop is operationally burdensome and error-prone, not a durable architectural fix.

quiz.question: A company's on-premises backup software writes data to virtual tapes. They want to move to AWS to reduce on-premises infrastructure while keeping the same backup application workflow with minimal changes. Which AWS service is MOST appropriate?
options: AWS DataSync with S3 Glacier as the destination
AWS Storage Gateway Tape Gateway
AWS Backup with cross-region replication
AWS Storage Gateway File Gateway mounted as a backup target
quiz.explanation: Storage Gateway Tape Gateway presents virtual tape libraries (VTL) to existing backup applications using industry-standard iSCSI, requiring no changes to the backup software. Virtual tapes are stored in S3 and archived to Glacier. DataSync is for file/object migration, not a VTL interface. AWS Backup manages backup policies but does not emulate a tape library. File Gateway presents NFS/SMB shares, not virtual tape drives.

quiz.question: A DevOps team is choosing between AWS Storage Gateway and AWS DataSync for their use case. Storage Gateway is being considered to extend on-premises storage to AWS, while DataSync is being considered for a one-time migration. Which statements correctly distinguish the two services? (Choose TWO)
options: Storage Gateway is designed for ongoing hybrid cloud active access to AWS storage
DataSync is designed to provide on-premises applications with persistent access to AWS file storage
DataSync is optimized for scheduled or one-time large-scale data transfer and migration
Storage Gateway can replace DataSync for petabyte-scale offline data transfers
Both services require AWS Direct Connect and cannot work over the public internet
quiz.explanation: Storage Gateway is a hybrid storage service for ongoing active workloads — it lets on-premises applications access S3, EBS, or Glacier through standard protocols (NFS, SMB, iSCSI). DataSync is a data transfer service optimized for scheduled, automated, or one-time bulk migrations between on-premises and AWS (or between AWS services). DataSync does not provide persistent active storage access. Storage Gateway cannot replace Snowball/Snowball Edge for offline petabyte-scale transfers. Both services can operate over the public internet, though Direct Connect is recommended for large or latency-sensitive transfers.

quiz.question: A startup wants to store infrequently accessed cold data at the LOWEST possible cost on AWS. Retrieval times of 12 hours are acceptable. Which S3 storage class should they choose?
options: S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval
S3 Glacier Deep Archive
S3 One Zone-IA
S3 Standard-IA
quiz.explanation: S3 Glacier Deep Archive is the lowest-cost AWS storage class, designed for data that is rarely (if ever) accessed and where retrieval within 12 hours is acceptable. Glacier Flexible Retrieval costs slightly more and offers faster retrieval options. S3 One Zone-IA is cheaper than Standard-IA but still significantly more expensive than Glacier tiers. Standard-IA is for data accessed monthly, not cold archival data.

quiz.question: An application on Linux EC2 instances in a single Availability Zone needs a shared file system where multiple instances can read and write simultaneously. The team also needs automatic data replication across AZs for durability. Which solution meets both requirements with LEAST operational overhead?
options: EBS io2 with Multi-Attach enabled across AZs
Amazon EFS with Regional storage class
Amazon FSx for Lustre in scratch mode
EBS gp3 volume with cross-AZ snapshot replication
quiz.explanation: Amazon EFS with the Regional (default) storage class automatically replicates data across multiple AZs, provides NFS-compatible concurrent multi-instance access, and requires no manual replication management — minimizing operational overhead. EBS Multi-Attach cannot span AZs and does not support concurrent file system writes without cluster coordination. FSx for Lustre in scratch mode has no data replication and is optimized for HPC, not general shared file storage. EBS snapshots replicate data but do not provide live shared file system access.

quiz.question: A company's EC2 instance needs 10,000 IOPS with consistent performance for a relational database. They want to avoid paying for provisioned IOPS while still getting predictable SSD performance. Which EBS volume type is MOST cost-effective?
options: io2 with 10,000 IOPS provisioned
gp3 with 10,000 IOPS configured
gp2 with a large enough volume to burst to 10,000 IOPS
st1 with IOPS boost enabled
quiz.explanation: gp3 allows independent configuration of up to 16,000 IOPS and 1,000 MB/s throughput at a flat per-GB price plus low incremental IOPS cost — significantly cheaper than io2 for most workloads under 16,000 IOPS. gp2 IOPS are tied to volume size (3 IOPS/GB), so getting 10,000 IOPS requires a 3,333 GB volume, which would be wasteful and expensive. io2 is more expensive and designed for workloads requiring >16,000 IOPS or higher durability SLAs. st1 is an HDD type with no IOPS boost — it is designed for throughput, not IOPS.

quiz.question: A solutions architect must recommend a storage solution for a new Linux application that requires: (1) shared file access from 20 EC2 instances across three AZs, and (2) the ability for multiple instances to write to the same files concurrently without application-level locking. (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon EFS is suitable because it natively supports concurrent NFS writes from multiple instances
EBS with Multi-Attach is suitable because it allows concurrent writes without additional configuration
Amazon EFS supports multi-AZ access, meeting the cross-AZ requirement
FSx for Windows File Server is suitable for Linux workloads requiring concurrent writes
EBS Multi-Attach supports concurrent file writes as long as a POSIX-compliant file system is used
quiz.explanation: EFS is a fully managed NFS file system that natively handles concurrent reads and writes from multiple Linux EC2 instances across multiple AZs — no application-level coordination is needed. EBS Multi-Attach explicitly does NOT support concurrent file system writes; the application must use a cluster-aware file system to coordinate access, and it is restricted to a single AZ. FSx for Windows is SMB-based and not suited for Linux workloads. POSIX compliance does not enable concurrent writes on EBS Multi-Attach volumes.

quiz.question: An enterprise has 500 TB of on-premises file data that must be migrated to Amazon S3 over the next two weeks. After migration, the on-premises systems will be decommissioned. Network bandwidth is 1 Gbps. Which approach should the solutions architect recommend?
options: Configure AWS Storage Gateway File Gateway and sync all data over the 1 Gbps link
Use AWS DataSync to transfer data over the existing 1 Gbps network connection
Order AWS Snowball Edge devices to physically transfer the data to AWS
Use S3 Transfer Acceleration with multipart upload over the 1 Gbps link
quiz.explanation: Transferring 500 TB over a 1 Gbps link would take approximately 45+ days even at full utilization — far exceeding the 2-week deadline. AWS Snowball Edge devices can transfer petabyte-scale data in days by physically shipping pre-loaded devices to AWS. DataSync and S3 Transfer Acceleration are both network-based and would not meet the 2-week window at 1 Gbps. Storage Gateway is for ongoing hybrid access, not bulk migration.

lessons.title: AWS Database Services
lessons.overview: Compare all AWS database types: relational (RDS, Aurora, Redshift), NoSQL (DynamoDB, DocumentDB), in-memory/caching (ElastiCache Redis vs Memcached), and purpose-built (Neptune, Timestream, QLDB, Keyspaces). Know when to use each.
lessons.overviewVi: So sánh tất cả các loại database AWS: quan hệ (RDS, Aurora, Redshift), NoSQL (DynamoDB, DocumentDB), in-memory/caching (ElastiCache Redis và Memcached), và chuyên biệt (Neptune, Timestream, QLDB, Keyspaces). Biết khi nào dùng loại nào.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: Database Categories
sections.contentHtml:
Relational (ACID)RDS, Aurora (OLTP) · Redshift (OLAP/data warehouse)
NoSQLDynamoDB (key-value/document) · DocumentDB (MongoDB-compatible)
In-MemoryElastiCache for Redis · ElastiCache for Memcached
SpecializedNeptune (graph) · Timestream (time series) · QLDB (ledger) · Keyspaces (Cassandra)
ACID = Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability — the four properties that guarantee transactional data integrity in relational databases.
sections.contentVi: Bốn loại database AWS: Quan hệ/ACID — RDS và Aurora cho OLTP (e-commerce, giao dịch thường xuyên); Redshift cho OLAP (phân tích, data warehouse). NoSQL — DynamoDB (key-value và document, schema linh hoạt); DocumentDB (tương thích MongoDB). In-Memory/Caching — ElastiCache Redis (có replication, high availability) và Memcached (đơn giản, không có replication). Chuyên biệt — Neptune (graph database), Timestream (time series), QLDB (ledger database), Keyspaces (Cassandra). ACID = Atomicity (tính nguyên tử), Consistency (nhất quán), Isolation (cô lập), Durability (bền vững) — bốn thuộc tính đảm bảo tính toàn vẹn giao dịch trong database quan hệ.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Amazon RDS & Amazon Aurora
sections.contentHtml:
Both are relational databases supporting OLTP workloads (e-commerce, transactions).

Amazon RDSManaged relational DB. Supports MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, Aurora. You choose EC2 instance type and storage. Single-AZ or Multi-AZ deployment.
Amazon AuroraFully managed by AWS. Compatible with MySQL and PostgreSQL. Auto-scales storage. Faster, cheaper, better than RDS. Deployed as a cluster (primary + Aurora replicas).

Multi-AZ vs Read Replica

Standby (Multi-AZ)Same region only. For high availability / automatic failover — NOT for read scaling
Read ReplicaCan be cross-region. For offloading read traffic from the primary instance
sections.contentVi: Cả hai đều là relational database hỗ trợ OLTP. Amazon RDS là managed relational DB hỗ trợ MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, Aurora. Bạn chọn loại EC2 instance và lưu trữ. Triển khai Single-AZ hoặc Multi-AZ. Amazon Aurora là fully managed bởi AWS, chỉ tương thích với MySQL và PostgreSQL, tự động scale lưu trữ, nhanh hơn và rẻ hơn RDS, triển khai dạng cluster (primary + Aurora replicas). Phân biệt Multi-AZ và Read Replica: Standby Multi-AZ — chỉ trong cùng region, dùng cho high availability và automatic failover, KHÔNG dùng để scale reads. Read Replica — có thể cross-region, dùng để offload read traffic từ primary instance. Đây là câu bẫy HAY xuất hiện trong đề thi.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Amazon Redshift — Data Warehouse
sections.contentHtml:
Amazon Redshift is a fully managed data warehouse for OLAP (analytical queries, reporting, BI tools).

  • Analyze large datasets using standard SQL and existing BI tools
  • Concurrency Scaling — virtually unlimited concurrent users and queries
  • Redshift Spectrum — query structured/semi-structured data in S3 without loading it into Redshift first
OLTP vs OLAP:
OLTP (RDS, Aurora) = frequent read/write transactions (e-commerce, banking)
OLAP (Redshift) = complex analytical queries on large historical datasets
sections.contentVi: Amazon Redshift là data warehouse được quản lý hoàn toàn dành cho OLAP (phân tích, báo cáo, BI tools). Phân tích tập dữ liệu lớn bằng SQL tiêu chuẩn và các công cụ BI hiện có. Concurrency Scaling — cho phép gần như vô số users và queries đồng thời. Redshift Spectrum — truy vấn dữ liệu có cấu trúc/bán cấu trúc trong S3 mà không cần load vào Redshift trước. Phân biệt OLTP và OLAP: OLTP (RDS, Aurora) = đọc/ghi thường xuyên (e-commerce, ngân hàng). OLAP (Redshift) = truy vấn phân tích phức tạp trên tập dữ liệu lịch sử lớn.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: DynamoDB & DocumentDB (NoSQL)
sections.contentHtml:
Amazon DynamoDBFully managed NoSQL (key-value + document). Tables, items (rows), attributes (columns). No rigid schema — add/remove attributes without affecting other tables. No table joins or foreign keys. Store complex hierarchical data within a single item.
Amazon DocumentDBFully managed MongoDB-compatible database. Stores, queries, and indexes JSON documents. Uses MongoDB terminology: collection (table), document (row), field (column).
NoSQL advantage: flexible schema — a relational DB change to one column can cascade failures; in DynamoDB you can safely add/remove attributes at any time.
sections.contentVi: Amazon DynamoDB là NoSQL được quản lý hoàn toàn (key-value + document). Có Tables, items (hàng) và attributes (cột). Không có schema cứng — có thể thêm/xóa attributes mà không ảnh hưởng items khác. Không có table joins hay foreign keys. Có thể lưu dữ liệu phân cấp phức tạp trong một item. Amazon DocumentDB là database tương thích MongoDB được quản lý hoàn toàn. Lưu trữ, truy vấn và index JSON documents. Dùng thuật ngữ MongoDB: collection (table), document (hàng), field (cột). Lợi thế NoSQL: schema linh hoạt — thay đổi một cột trong relational DB có thể gây cascade failures; trong DynamoDB bạn có thể thêm/xóa attributes bất cứ lúc nào an toàn.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Amazon ElastiCache — In-Memory Caching
sections.contentHtml:
ElastiCache reduces database load by caching frequently repeated queries in-memory (sub-millisecond latency).

ElastiCache for MemcachedSimple, scalable caching layer. Multi-threaded (utilizes multiple CPU cores). No data replication — lower availability. Best for simple caching of data-intensive apps.
ElastiCache for RedisAdvanced features: data replication, cluster mode (multi-AZ), PUB/SUB messaging, geospatial, point-in-time snapshots. Best when high availability is needed.
Key difference: Redis has data replication (HA) — Memcached does NOT. If the question mentions high availability or failover for cache → choose Redis.
sections.contentVi: ElastiCache giảm tải database bằng cách cache các query lặp lại thường xuyên trong bộ nhớ (độ trễ dưới millisecond). ElastiCache for Memcached: caching layer đơn giản, có thể mở rộng, multi-threaded (tận dụng nhiều CPU cores), KHÔNG có data replication — tính sẵn sàng thấp hơn. Tốt nhất cho caching đơn giản. ElastiCache for Redis: tính năng nâng cao — data replication, cluster mode (multi-AZ), PUB/SUB messaging, geospatial, point-in-time snapshots. Tốt nhất khi cần high availability cho cache. Điểm khác biệt then chốt: Redis CÓ data replication (HA) — Memcached KHÔNG CÓ. Câu hỏi nhắc high availability hoặc failover cho cache → chọn Redis.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Purpose-Built Databases
sections.contentHtml:
Amazon KeyspacesApache Cassandra-compatible. Serverless, scalable wide-column store for large datasets. No server management.
Amazon NeptuneGraph database. Store billions of relationships, query with millisecond latency. Uses nodes (entities) and edges (relationships).
Amazon TimestreamTime series database. Serverless, for IoT and operational apps. Track stock prices, CPU utilization, sensor readings over time.
Amazon QLDBLedger database. Provides a transparent, immutable, cryptographically verifiable transaction log. Audit every change ever made to application data.
sections.contentVi: Các database chuyên biệt: Amazon Keyspaces tương thích Apache Cassandra — serverless, scalable wide-column store cho tập dữ liệu lớn, không cần quản lý server. Amazon Neptune là graph database — lưu hàng tỷ mối quan hệ, truy vấn với độ trễ millisecond, dùng nodes (thực thể) và edges (mối quan hệ), lý tưởng cho mạng xã hội, phát hiện gian lận. Amazon Timestream là time series database serverless dành cho IoT và ứng dụng vận hành — theo dõi giá cổ phiếu, sử dụng CPU, số liệu cảm biến theo thời gian. Amazon QLDB là ledger database — cung cấp transaction log minh bạch, bất biến, có thể verify bằng mật mã, ghi lại và không thể thay đổi mọi thay đổi dữ liệu, dùng cho audit tài chính.

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Database Selection Guide
sections.contentHtml:
• OLTP, ACID, relational? → RDS or Aurora (Aurora = better performance)
  • Data warehouse, analytics, BI? → Redshift (OLAP)
  • NoSQL, flexible schema, millisecond latency? → DynamoDB
  • MongoDB-compatible, JSON documents? → DocumentDB
  • In-memory cache, high availability? → ElastiCache for Redis
  • In-memory cache, simple, multi-threaded? → ElastiCache for Memcached
  • Graph database (social networks, relationships)? → Neptune
  • Time series (IoT, metrics over time)? → Timestream
  • Immutable audit log? → QLDB
  • Apache Cassandra workloads? → Keyspaces
  • RDS Multi-AZ standby ≠ read scaling (that's read replica)
sections.contentVi: Bảng chọn database nhanh: OLTP, ACID, quan hệ → RDS hoặc Aurora (Aurora = hiệu năng tốt hơn). Data warehouse, phân tích, BI → Redshift (OLAP). NoSQL, schema linh hoạt, độ trễ millisecond → DynamoDB. Tương thích MongoDB, JSON documents → DocumentDB. In-memory cache, high availability → ElastiCache Redis. In-memory cache, đơn giản, multi-threaded → ElastiCache Memcached. Graph database (mạng xã hội, quan hệ phức tạp) → Neptune. Time series (IoT, metrics theo thời gian) → Timestream. Audit log bất biến → QLDB. Apache Cassandra workloads → Keyspaces. Nhớ quan trọng: RDS Multi-AZ standby ≠ scale reads — đó là read replica!

summary: RDS = managed relational DB (multiple engines). Aurora = fully managed MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible, faster and cheaper than RDS
Multi-AZ standby = high availability (same region, auto failover). Read replica = read scaling (can be cross-region) — NOT interchangeable
Redshift = OLAP data warehouse (complex analytics/BI). RDS/Aurora = OLTP (frequent transactions like e-commerce)
DynamoDB = NoSQL, no rigid schema, no joins. DocumentDB = MongoDB-compatible JSON document store
ElastiCache Redis = in-memory cache WITH replication (HA). Memcached = simple cache WITHOUT replication
Specialized: Neptune (graph), Timestream (time series/IoT), QLDB (immutable ledger/audit), Keyspaces (Cassandra)
summaryVi: RDS = managed relational DB (nhiều engine). Aurora = fully managed MySQL/PostgreSQL, nhanh hơn và rẻ hơn RDS
Multi-AZ standby = high availability (cùng region, auto failover). Read replica = scale reads (có thể cross-region) — KHÔNG giống nhau
Redshift = OLAP data warehouse (phân tích/BI phức tạp). RDS/Aurora = OLTP (giao dịch thường xuyên như e-commerce)
DynamoDB = NoSQL, không rigid schema, không joins. DocumentDB = tương thích MongoDB, lưu JSON document
ElastiCache Redis = in-memory cache CÓ replication (HA). Memcached = cache đơn giản KHÔNG CÓ replication
Chuyên biệt: Neptune (graph), Timestream (time series/IoT), QLDB (ledger bất biến/audit), Keyspaces (Cassandra)

quiz.question: An e-commerce platform runs a MySQL database on EC2 instances. The team spends significant time patching OS updates, managing backups, and handling failover manually. They want to reduce this operational burden while keeping MySQL compatibility. Which solution offers the LEAST operational overhead?
options: Migrate to Amazon RDS for MySQL with Multi-AZ enabled
Move the database to a larger EC2 instance with automated AMI backups
Use Amazon DynamoDB and rewrite the application to use NoSQL
Deploy MySQL on ECS Fargate containers with automated task restarts
quiz.explanation: Amazon RDS for MySQL is a fully managed service: AWS handles OS patching, automated backups, and Multi-AZ failover. EC2 self-managed still requires manual patching. DynamoDB would require a full application rewrite. Fargate containers don't eliminate DB operational burden.

quiz.question: A SaaS company is choosing between Amazon RDS Aurora and Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL for their new flagship product. Aurora PostgreSQL costs slightly more per instance but the team expects significant read traffic. Which statement BEST justifies choosing Aurora over standard RDS PostgreSQL?
options: Aurora provides up to 5x throughput improvement over standard PostgreSQL and supports up to 15 read replicas with sub-10ms replica lag
Aurora is the only RDS option that supports automated backups and point-in-time recovery
Aurora automatically partitions tables across multiple Availability Zones, which RDS PostgreSQL cannot do
Aurora is cheaper than standard RDS PostgreSQL at all storage tiers
quiz.explanation: Aurora's key advantage is performance (up to 5x over MySQL, up to 3x over PostgreSQL) and support for up to 15 read replicas with very low replica lag — ideal for high read traffic. Both RDS and Aurora support automated backups and point-in-time recovery. Aurora is not universally cheaper.

quiz.question: A financial services application requires its production database to automatically recover from an Availability Zone failure within 60 seconds, with no manual intervention. The database must remain in the same AWS Region. Which RDS feature DIRECTLY addresses this requirement?
options: RDS Multi-AZ deployment with synchronous replication to a standby instance
RDS Read Replica promoted to primary in the event of failure
RDS automated snapshot restored to a new instance in a different AZ
RDS cross-region replication with automatic DNS failover
quiz.explanation: Multi-AZ maintains a synchronous standby in a different AZ and performs automatic DNS-level failover — typically within 60–120 seconds — with no manual steps. Read Replicas use asynchronous replication and require manual promotion. Snapshot restore takes much longer. Cross-region replication is outside the same-region requirement.

quiz.question: A media company's reporting dashboard generates complex SQL joins across hundreds of millions of rows of historical viewing data. The queries are slow on their RDS PostgreSQL instance, which also serves live transactional traffic. What is the MOST appropriate AWS service to offload these analytics queries?
options: Amazon Redshift
Amazon ElastiCache for Redis
Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL with additional read replicas
Amazon DynamoDB with DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX)
quiz.explanation: Amazon Redshift is purpose-built for OLAP workloads — complex analytical queries across large datasets using columnar storage. RDS/Aurora is optimized for OLTP (frequent, small transactions). ElastiCache caches results but can't run complex SQL analytics. DynamoDB+DAX is for NoSQL key-value access patterns.

quiz.question: A startup is building a real-time leaderboard for a mobile game. The leaderboard must handle millions of requests per second with single-digit millisecond latency, and the data model is a simple ranked list with player scores — no complex joins required. Which database service is the BEST fit?
options: Amazon DynamoDB
Amazon RDS Aurora MySQL
Amazon Redshift
Amazon QLDB
quiz.explanation: DynamoDB is purpose-built for massive scale, high-throughput NoSQL workloads with consistent single-digit millisecond latency. A leaderboard is a simple key-value pattern with no relational joins. Aurora MySQL handles relational OLTP but doesn't scale to millions of RPS as seamlessly. Redshift is for analytics. QLDB is for immutable audit ledgers.

quiz.question: A development team is deciding between Amazon ElastiCache for Redis and ElastiCache for Memcached for their session store. Their requirements include: automatic failover if the cache node fails, the ability to store complex data structures like sorted sets, and sub-millisecond response times. Which TWO characteristics describe Redis that fulfill these requirements? (Choose TWO)
options: Redis supports replication and automatic failover through Multi-AZ with automatic failover enabled
Redis supports complex data structures such as sorted sets, lists, and hashes
Memcached supports replication across multiple AZs natively
Redis is a multi-threaded architecture designed for simpler horizontal scaling
Memcached supports persistence and snapshot-based backups
quiz.explanation: Redis uniquely supports replication with automatic failover (via Redis Sentinel/Multi-AZ) and rich data structures (sorted sets for leaderboards, hashes for sessions, etc.). Memcached does NOT support replication or persistence — it is a simple, multi-threaded cache. Redis is single-threaded but feature-rich.

quiz.question: A company stores IoT sensor readings every second from 10,000 devices. They need to query historical trends, detect anomalies over time windows, and keep data for 2 years. The data has a clear timestamp dimension. Which AWS database service is MOST purpose-built for this use case?
options: Amazon Timestream
Amazon DynamoDB
Amazon RDS Aurora PostgreSQL
Amazon Redshift
quiz.explanation: Amazon Timestream is a serverless time series database specifically designed for IoT and operational data — it stores and queries time-stamped data efficiently with built-in time series functions. DynamoDB can store time series but lacks built-in time analytics. RDS and Redshift can work but require custom modeling; Timestream is the purpose-built, lowest-overhead choice.

quiz.question: A bank must maintain an immutable, verifiable record of every account balance change for regulatory auditing purposes. The system must be able to prove that no historical record has been altered or deleted. Which AWS service is DESIGNED for this requirement?
options: Amazon QLDB (Quantum Ledger Database)
Amazon DynamoDB with point-in-time recovery enabled
Amazon RDS with automated backups and audit logging
Amazon S3 with Object Lock in Compliance mode
quiz.explanation: Amazon QLDB is specifically designed as an immutable ledger database — every change is cryptographically verifiable through a journal, and history cannot be altered or deleted. DynamoDB PITR allows restores but doesn't provide cryptographic proof of immutability. RDS audit logs are mutable. S3 Object Lock prevents deletion but isn't a queryable database.

quiz.question: A social network needs to find all 2nd- and 3rd-degree connections between users (friends of friends), identify mutual connections, and recommend new friends based on relationship paths. The dataset has billions of relationships. Which AWS database service is the BEST fit?
options: Amazon Neptune
Amazon DynamoDB
Amazon RDS Aurora
Amazon DocumentDB
quiz.explanation: Amazon Neptune is a managed graph database that natively supports graph traversal queries (Gremlin, SPARQL, openCypher), making relationship-path queries extremely efficient. DynamoDB and RDS/Aurora are not designed for graph traversal — these queries would require complex, slow application-level joins. DocumentDB is for JSON document storage.

quiz.question: A company is migrating a content management system that currently uses MongoDB. They need a fully managed AWS service that maintains compatibility with their existing MongoDB drivers and supports flexible JSON document storage with indexing. They also need the solution to be highly available across multiple AZs. Which TWO statements are true about Amazon DocumentDB? (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon DocumentDB is compatible with MongoDB drivers and tools
Amazon DocumentDB stores data as relational tables with foreign key constraints
Amazon DocumentDB automatically replicates data across multiple AZs for high availability
Amazon DocumentDB is a key-value store optimized for single-digit millisecond reads
Amazon DocumentDB requires you to manage the underlying EC2 instances and storage
quiz.explanation: DocumentDB is MongoDB-compatible (works with existing MongoDB 3.6/4.0 drivers) and provides managed high availability by automatically replicating storage across three AZs. It is not relational, not a pure key-value store, and is fully managed — you don't manage EC2 or storage.

quiz.question: An e-commerce platform uses RDS MySQL for orders and inventory. The team wants to offload read-heavy reporting queries (last 30 days of orders, inventory levels) from the primary instance without impacting transactional performance. Which approach is MOST cost-effective with the LEAST operational overhead?
options: Create one or more RDS Read Replicas and point reporting queries to the replica endpoint
Enable Multi-AZ on the existing RDS instance and send read traffic to the standby
Migrate the reporting workload to Redshift using daily exports from RDS
Add an ElastiCache cluster in front of all reporting queries
quiz.explanation: RDS Read Replicas are designed specifically for read scaling — reporting queries run against the replica, leaving the primary free for writes. Multi-AZ standby instances are NOT available for reads — they exist only for failover. Redshift migration adds significant overhead for simple reporting. ElastiCache caches static results but cannot execute dynamic SQL queries.

quiz.question: A global gaming company wants to create an RDS Read Replica to serve users in Europe from a US-East primary database, reducing latency for European read queries. They also want the replica to be able to serve as a standalone database for European users if needed. Which capability makes this possible?
options: Cross-region Read Replicas, which can be promoted to standalone DB instances
Multi-AZ standby instances deployed in EU regions
Aurora Global Database with write forwarding enabled in all regions
RDS automated backups restored into the EU region
quiz.explanation: RDS Read Replicas support cross-region deployment, reducing read latency for geographically distributed users, and can be manually promoted to standalone instances. Multi-AZ standbys are within the same region only and cannot serve reads. Aurora Global Database is valid but targets Aurora specifically, not standard RDS.

quiz.question: A company runs an Apache Cassandra cluster on EC2 for their user preference store. The team wants to migrate to a fully managed service to reduce operational work, while keeping Cassandra Query Language (CQL) compatibility. Which TWO statements correctly describe Amazon Keyspaces? (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon Keyspaces is compatible with Apache Cassandra and supports CQL
Amazon Keyspaces requires managing the underlying Cassandra nodes and data replication
Amazon Keyspaces is a serverless, fully managed service that automatically scales
Amazon Keyspaces is a graph database that stores relationship data
Amazon Keyspaces does not support CQL and requires a schema migration to DynamoDB
quiz.explanation: Amazon Keyspaces (for Apache Cassandra) is fully managed and CQL-compatible, so existing Cassandra applications work with minimal changes. It is serverless — no node management, automatic scaling. It is not a graph database and does not require migration away from CQL.

quiz.question: A startup's application currently uses an RDS PostgreSQL database for both transactional (OLTP) operations and large quarterly analytics reports. During the quarterly report run, database CPU hits 95% and response times for the live application degrade severely. What is the MOST appropriate architectural change with the LEAST code refactoring?
options: Create a Read Replica dedicated to analytics queries and route report queries to that endpoint
Enable Multi-AZ and run analytics queries against the standby instance
Move the entire database to Redshift, which handles both OLTP and OLAP
Increase the RDS instance type to the largest available instance class
quiz.explanation: A dedicated Read Replica for analytics offloads heavy queries from the primary with minimal code change (just update the connection string for reports). Multi-AZ standbys cannot serve read traffic. Redshift is OLAP-only and requires significant refactoring to handle OLTP. Scaling up the instance doesn't address workload isolation.

quiz.question: A company's DynamoDB table holds hundreds of millions of items. Queries by non-primary key attributes (e.g., status, category) are becoming slow because they require full table scans. The team wants to efficiently query by these attributes with minimal changes to the data model. What should they do?
options: Create Global Secondary Indexes (GSIs) on the frequently queried attributes
Migrate the table to Amazon RDS Aurora where SQL indexes can be added freely
Enable DynamoDB Streams and process queries through a Lambda fan-out pattern
Add ElastiCache in front of DynamoDB and cache query results by attribute value
quiz.explanation: Global Secondary Indexes in DynamoDB allow efficient queries on non-primary-key attributes without full table scans — exactly what's needed here. Migrating to Aurora would require redesigning the data model. DynamoDB Streams is for change capture, not query optimization. ElastiCache caches results but doesn't help with ad-hoc attribute queries.

quiz.question: A company is evaluating database services for a new business intelligence platform that ingests 500 GB of sales data daily and runs overnight batch queries with complex multi-table joins and aggregations used to generate executive dashboards. The queries do not need to run in real time. Which service is MOST suitable?
options: Amazon Redshift with columnar storage and distribution keys optimized for query patterns
Amazon Aurora MySQL with read replicas for BI tool connections
Amazon DynamoDB with on-demand capacity and DynamoDB Accelerator
Amazon RDS PostgreSQL with tablespace partitioning
quiz.explanation: Redshift is the definitive OLAP data warehouse on AWS — columnar storage dramatically accelerates aggregate queries on large datasets, and distribution keys minimize data movement during joins. Aurora/RDS are OLTP systems not optimized for this workload at scale. DynamoDB is NoSQL and lacks complex JOIN/aggregation support.

quiz.question: An architect is designing a highly available caching layer for a web application. The cache must survive a single node failure without losing all cached data, support atomic operations on counters, and be able to persist cache data to disk for faster recovery after a full restart. Which TWO features make Amazon ElastiCache for Redis the correct choice over Memcached for this scenario? (Choose TWO)
options: Redis supports replication — data is replicated to replica nodes, surviving single node failures
Memcached natively supports disk persistence and snapshot-based recovery
Redis supports persistence (RDB snapshots and AOF logging) enabling faster recovery after restart
Memcached supports atomic increment/decrement operations on complex data structures
Redis and Memcached are architecturally identical; the choice is based on instance size only
quiz.explanation: Redis uniquely provides replication (data survives node failures) and persistence (RDB/AOF) for faster warm restarts. Memcached is an in-memory cache with no replication and no persistence — all data is lost if the node restarts. Both support basic atomic counter operations, but only Redis has the HA and persistence features required here.

lessons.title: AWS Deployment Services
lessons.overview: Learn AWS deployment services — from CloudFormation's infrastructure-as-code to Elastic Beanstalk's PaaS model, CodeDeploy's application rollouts, and container orchestration with ECS and EKS. A critical SAA topic: know which services create resources versus which only deploy to existing ones.
lessons.overviewVi: Tìm hiểu các dịch vụ triển khai AWS — từ CloudFormation (IaC) đến Elastic Beanstalk (PaaS), CodeDeploy (deploy ứng dụng) và điều phối container với ECS/EKS. Đây là chủ đề quan trọng trong SAA: phân biệt dịch vụ tạo resource mới và dịch vụ chỉ deploy lên resource có sẵn.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: From Manual to Automated Deployment
sections.contentHtml:
Traditionally, deployment meant manually copying files, configuring web servers (Apache, IIS), and provisioning hardware — a process prone to human error that could take weeks or months. AWS deployment services eliminate this friction.

Some use Infrastructure as Code (IaC): you provide a text-based definition file and AWS automatically provisions all required resources in one click. AWS supports cloud-only, hybrid (on-prem + cloud), and multi-cloud (AWS + Azure + GCP) deployments.

Services coveredCloudFormation, Elastic Beanstalk, CodeDeploy, ECS, EKS, OpsWorks, Proton
IaC definitionManaging and provisioning servers, databases, CDNs via machine-readable definition files
sections.contentVi: Trước đây, deploy phải sao chép file thủ công, cấu hình web server và cung cấp phần cứng — dễ lỗi, mất hàng tuần đến tháng. AWS deployment services loại bỏ sự phức tạp này. Một số dùng IaC (Infrastructure as Code): bạn cung cấp file text định nghĩa hạ tầng, AWS tự động tạo toàn bộ resource chỉ với một click. AWS hỗ trợ: cloud-only, hybrid (on-prem + cloud), multi-cloud (AWS + Azure + GCP). Các dịch vụ trong bài: CloudFormation, Elastic Beanstalk, CodeDeploy, ECS, EKS, OpsWorks, Proton.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: AWS CloudFormation: Infrastructure as Code
sections.contentHtml:
AWS CloudFormation is the primary IaC service in AWS. You define infrastructure in a YAML or JSON template; CloudFormation provisions all specified resources automatically.

StackA group of AWS resources defined by one template — represents your full or partial architecture
Nested StackRoot stack with child stacks per tier (presentation, application, data) — loosely coupled, independently manageable
Change SetDry-run mode — preview how changes affect running resources before deploying them
StackSetDeploy, update, or delete stacks across multiple AWS accounts and regions in a single operation
DesignerDrag-and-drop graphic tool for creating and modifying CloudFormation templates visually

Extensions of CloudFormation:

  • AWS CDK — open-source SDK; define infrastructure programmatically using TypeScript, Python, Java, or .NET
  • AWS SAM — extends CloudFormation for serverless apps (Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway); SAM template = CloudFormation template + serverless components
  • Serverless Application Repository — publish, share, and deploy serverless apps publicly or privately within your org
sections.contentVi: AWS CloudFormation là dịch vụ IaC chính của AWS. Bạn định nghĩa hạ tầng trong template YAML hoặc JSON; CloudFormation tự động tạo toàn bộ resource được chỉ định. Stack: nhóm AWS resource từ một template, đại diện cho toàn bộ hoặc một phần kiến trúc. Nested Stack: root stack với các child stack theo từng tầng (presentation, application, data) — loosely coupled, quản lý độc lập. Change Set: dry-run — xem trước thay đổi ảnh hưởng đến resource đang chạy trước khi deploy. StackSet: deploy/update/xóa stacks trên nhiều AWS account và region trong một thao tác. Designer: công cụ kéo-thả tạo và sửa template. Mở rộng CloudFormation: AWS CDK — SDK open-source định nghĩa hạ tầng bằng TypeScript/Python/Java/.NET; AWS SAM — mở rộng CloudFormation cho serverless (Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway); Serverless Application Repository — publish và chia sẻ serverless apps.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Elastic Beanstalk and CodeDeploy
sections.contentHtml:
AWS Elastic Beanstalk is a managed platform (PaaS) — upload your code and it automatically provisions the required cloud environment.

  • Supports: Java, .NET, PHP, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, Docker
  • Web server environment — runs static websites, web apps, or web APIs serving HTTP requests
  • Worker environment — processes long-running workloads on demand; supports scheduled tasks; integrates with Amazon SQS
  • Config files stored in the .ebextensions folder

AWS CodeDeploy is a fully managed deployment service — but only deploys to existing compute resources.

Deploy targetsEC2 instances, ECS clusters, Lambda functions, on-premises servers
Key constraintDoes NOT create AWS resources — application deployments only to existing compute
Key distinction: CloudFormation, SAM, and Elastic Beanstalk can provision new resources. CodeDeploy cannot — resources must already exist.
sections.contentVi: AWS Elastic Beanstalk là nền tảng quản lý (PaaS) — upload code, dịch vụ tự động tạo môi trường cloud cần thiết. Hỗ trợ: Java, .NET, PHP, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, Docker. Web server environment: chạy website tĩnh, web app hoặc web API phục vụ HTTP request. Worker environment: xử lý workload chạy lâu theo yêu cầu, hỗ trợ scheduled task, tích hợp Amazon SQS. File cấu hình lưu trong thư mục .ebextensions. AWS CodeDeploy là dịch vụ deploy tự động — nhưng CHỈ deploy lên resource đã có sẵn. Đối tượng: EC2 instances, ECS clusters, Lambda functions, on-premises servers. Điểm mấu chốt: CodeDeploy KHÔNG tạo AWS resource mới — chỉ deploy ứng dụng lên compute đã tồn tại. Phân biệt: CloudFormation/SAM/Elastic Beanstalk có thể tạo resource mới; CodeDeploy thì không.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Container Orchestration: ECS and EKS
sections.contentHtml:
Container orchestration automates provisioning, deploying, scaling, and networking of containerized workloads.

Amazon ECS — fully managed Docker container orchestration service.

ECS on EC2 (VPC)Launch containers on EC2 instances in your VPC — you manage the EC2 fleet; use CloudWatch Container Insights to monitor
ECS on FargateServerless — AWS fully manages compute; significantly reduces operating costs
ECS AnywhereRun ECS on your own on-premises infrastructure; same cluster management, workload scheduling, and monitoring features

Amazon EKS — managed Kubernetes service; like ECS but for Kubernetes clusters.

EKS Cluster (AWS)Kubernetes on managed or self-managed EC2 nodes, or on Fargate (serverless)
EKS on OutpostsPhysical AWS Outpost rack on-premises; data plane on-premises for more control
EKS AnywhereRun Kubernetes entirely on your own hardware — with AWS support
EKS DistroOpen-source distribution of EKS Kubernetes software; deploy on own hardware — NO AWS support
Memory tip: ECS = Docker; EKS = Kubernetes. Both support Fargate (serverless) and "Anywhere" (on-premises with AWS support).
sections.contentVi: Container orchestration tự động hóa provisioning, deploy, scale và networking cho containerized workloads. Amazon ECS — điều phối Docker container được quản lý hoàn toàn. ECS trên EC2 (VPC): chạy container trên EC2 instances trong VPC, bạn quản lý EC2 fleet, dùng CloudWatch Container Insights để monitor. ECS trên Fargate: serverless — AWS quản lý toàn bộ compute, giảm chi phí vận hành đáng kể. ECS Anywhere: chạy ECS trên hạ tầng on-premises của bạn, đầy đủ tính năng quản lý cluster và monitoring như trên cloud. Amazon EKS — dịch vụ Kubernetes được quản lý; giống ECS nhưng cho Kubernetes cluster. EKS Cluster (trên AWS): Kubernetes trên EC2 nodes hoặc Fargate. EKS on Outposts: rack AWS Outpost vật lý tại chỗ, data plane on-premises. EKS Anywhere: Kubernetes hoàn toàn trên phần cứng riêng — có hỗ trợ AWS. EKS Distro: phân phối open-source, deploy trên phần cứng riêng — KHÔNG có hỗ trợ AWS. Ghi nhớ: ECS = Docker; EKS = Kubernetes. Cả hai hỗ trợ Fargate và Anywhere.

sections.label: Deep Dive
sections.heading: OpsWorks and Proton
sections.contentHtml:
AWS OpsWorks provides managed instances of Chef and Puppet automation platforms (both are IaC tools that automate server provisioning and configuration).

  • OpsWorks for Chef Automate
  • OpsWorks for Puppet Enterprise
  • OpsWorks Stacks

AWS Proton automates container and serverless deployments while enforcing consistent standards across large developer teams.

  • Platform teams create pre-approved stacks with guardrails
  • Developers deploy via a self-service portal using those pre-approved stacks
  • Proton templates contain all info needed to deploy environments and services
  • Proton components extend service templates for additional flexibility
sections.contentVi: AWS OpsWorks cung cấp managed instances cho nền tảng tự động hóa Chef và Puppet (cả hai là IaC tools tự động hóa provisioning và cấu hình server). Ba phiên bản: OpsWorks for Chef Automate, OpsWorks for Puppet Enterprise, OpsWorks Stacks. AWS Proton tự động hóa deploy container và serverless, đồng thời đảm bảo tiêu chuẩn nhất quán cho team developer lớn. Platform team tạo pre-approved stacks với guardrails; developer deploy qua self-service portal. Proton template chứa toàn bộ thông tin để deploy môi trường và service. Proton components mở rộng service template để linh hoạt hơn.

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Deployment Services: Key Exam Distinctions
sections.contentHtml:
• CloudFormation = primary IaC; creates resources from YAML/JSON templates; Change Set = dry-run preview
  • StackSet = deploy CloudFormation across multiple accounts and regions
  • CodeDeploy = deploys application code to EXISTING compute only; does NOT create resources
  • Elastic Beanstalk = PaaS; upload code → environment auto-provisioned; worker env integrates with SQS
  • ECS = Docker; EKS = Kubernetes; both support Fargate + Anywhere
  • EKS Distro = open-source, no AWS support; EKS Anywhere = on-premises with AWS support
  • OpsWorks = managed Chef and Puppet
  • SAM = CloudFormation extension for serverless (Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway)
Classic trap: "Deploy to existing EC2" → CodeDeploy. "Provision and deploy infrastructure" → CloudFormation or Elastic Beanstalk.
sections.contentVi: Phân biệt quan trọng trong thi: CloudFormation = IaC chính, tạo resource từ YAML/JSON; Change Set = dry-run preview trước khi deploy. StackSet = deploy CloudFormation trên nhiều account và region. CodeDeploy = chỉ deploy lên compute ĐÃ CÓ; không tạo resource mới. Elastic Beanstalk = PaaS; upload code → tự động tạo môi trường; worker env tích hợp SQS. ECS = Docker; EKS = Kubernetes; cả hai hỗ trợ Fargate và Anywhere. EKS Distro = open-source, không có hỗ trợ AWS; EKS Anywhere = on-premises có hỗ trợ AWS. OpsWorks = quản lý Chef và Puppet. SAM = CloudFormation mở rộng cho serverless. Bẫy phổ biến: 'deploy lên EC2 có sẵn' → CodeDeploy; 'cung cấp và deploy hạ tầng' → CloudFormation hoặc Elastic Beanstalk.

summary: IaC (Infrastructure as Code) automates provisioning via machine-readable definition files — AWS CloudFormation is the primary IaC service using YAML/JSON templates
CloudFormation key features: Stack (resource group), Nested Stack (loosely coupled tiers), Change Set (dry-run preview), StackSet (multi-account/region); CDK and SAM extend it
CodeDeploy only deploys application code to EXISTING compute resources (EC2, ECS, Lambda, on-premises) — it does NOT provision new AWS resources
ECS (Docker) and EKS (Kubernetes) both support three modes: EC2 in VPC, Fargate (serverless), and Anywhere (on-premises); EKS also has Outposts and Distro options
EKS Distro = open-source Kubernetes, no AWS support; EKS Anywhere = on-premises with AWS support; OpsWorks = managed Chef/Puppet
summaryVi: IaC tự động hóa provisioning qua file định nghĩa — AWS CloudFormation là dịch vụ IaC chính, dùng template YAML/JSON
Tính năng CloudFormation: Stack (nhóm resource), Nested Stack (loosely coupled theo tầng), Change Set (dry-run preview), StackSet (nhiều account/region); CDK và SAM mở rộng CloudFormation
CodeDeploy chỉ deploy code lên compute ĐÃ TỒN TẠI (EC2, ECS, Lambda, on-premises) — không tạo AWS resource mới
ECS (Docker) và EKS (Kubernetes) đều hỗ trợ 3 chế độ: EC2 trong VPC, Fargate (serverless) và Anywhere (on-premises); EKS có thêm Outposts và Distro
EKS Distro = Kubernetes open-source, không có hỗ trợ AWS; EKS Anywhere = on-premises có hỗ trợ AWS; OpsWorks = quản lý Chef/Puppet

quiz.question: A company wants to provision a multi-tier application stack (VPC, subnets, EC2 instances, RDS) across three AWS regions consistently. The operations team needs to be able to preview changes before applying them. Which approach provides the LEAST operational overhead?
options: Use AWS CloudFormation templates with Change Sets to preview and deploy resources across all three regions
Use AWS CodeDeploy to push the application code to pre-existing EC2 instances in each region
Manually provision resources in each region using the AWS Console, then document the configuration
Use AWS OpsWorks with Chef recipes to configure servers after manual provisioning
quiz.explanation: CloudFormation automates provisioning of all AWS resources from YAML/JSON templates, and Change Sets allow dry-run previews before applying changes — exactly meeting both requirements. CodeDeploy only deploys application code to EXISTING resources and cannot provision infrastructure. Manual provisioning has high operational overhead, and OpsWorks manages configuration on already-provisioned servers.

quiz.question: A startup is running containerized microservices and wants to avoid managing underlying servers entirely. They need a solution that scales automatically and minimizes infrastructure overhead. Which deployment option is MOST cost-effective for unpredictable, bursty workloads?
options: ECS with EC2 launch type in a VPC, with Auto Scaling Groups
EKS with self-managed node groups on EC2 instances
ECS with Fargate launch type
ECS with EC2 Spot Instances managed via OpsWorks
quiz.explanation: ECS Fargate is serverless — AWS manages the underlying compute, so there are no EC2 instances to provision, patch, or scale. For bursty workloads this avoids paying for idle capacity. EC2-based options (ECS EC2, EKS with node groups) require managing and right-sizing instances. OpsWorks adds configuration management overhead on top of EC2.

quiz.question: A development team uses AWS CDK to define their infrastructure. During a deployment review, they want to see exactly which resources will be created, modified, or deleted before changes go live. Which CloudFormation feature should they use?
options: StackSet
Nested Stack
Change Set
Drift Detection
quiz.explanation: A Change Set is CloudFormation's dry-run preview that shows exactly which resources will be added, modified, or removed before the stack update is executed. StackSets deploy stacks across multiple accounts/regions. Nested Stacks allow loosely coupled tier composition. Drift Detection identifies manual changes to resources but does not preview planned updates.

quiz.question: A company has a large CloudFormation template that has grown difficult to maintain. The network team manages VPC resources and the application team manages EC2/RDS resources separately. Which CloudFormation feature allows each team to manage their own template while keeping them linked?
options: CloudFormation StackSets
CloudFormation Nested Stacks
AWS CodeDeploy deployment groups
CloudFormation Change Sets
quiz.explanation: Nested Stacks allow a parent stack to reference child stacks, enabling loosely coupled tiers where each team can own and update their template independently. StackSets are for deploying the same stack across multiple accounts/regions. CodeDeploy handles application code deployment, not infrastructure composition. Change Sets are for previewing updates.

quiz.question: An enterprise runs an application on EC2 instances and needs to deploy a new version of the application binary to hundreds of instances with zero downtime. The infrastructure already exists. Which AWS service is designed specifically for this use case?
options: AWS CloudFormation with a rolling update policy
AWS CodeDeploy with an in-place or blue/green deployment
AWS OpsWorks with Chef cookbooks
AWS CDK with a hotswap deployment
quiz.explanation: CodeDeploy is purpose-built to deploy application code to EXISTING compute resources (EC2, Lambda, ECS, on-premises) using in-place or blue/green strategies to achieve zero downtime. CloudFormation provisions infrastructure; while it can trigger deployments, CodeDeploy is the correct specialized tool. OpsWorks manages configuration management workflows, not zero-downtime code deployments.

quiz.question: A company needs to run Kubernetes workloads on-premises due to data residency requirements, while still benefiting from AWS tooling and support. Which TWO EKS options support running Kubernetes on-premises? (Choose TWO)
options: EKS on Fargate
EKS Anywhere
EKS Distro
EKS on EC2 in a VPC
EKS on Outposts
quiz.explanation: EKS Anywhere allows running Kubernetes on-premises using AWS-supported tooling and lifecycle management. EKS Distro is the open-source Kubernetes distribution that powers EKS, which teams can run on-premises (though without AWS support). EKS Fargate and EC2 in a VPC run in AWS regions. EKS on Outposts runs AWS infrastructure physically in your data center but is an AWS-managed Outpost rack, not a purely on-premises deployment.

quiz.question: A solutions architect needs to deploy the same CloudFormation stack to 50 AWS accounts across 5 regions as part of a multi-account organization setup. Which CloudFormation feature is MOST appropriate?
options: CloudFormation Nested Stacks with cross-stack references
CloudFormation StackSets with AWS Organizations integration
CloudFormation Change Sets deployed sequentially per account
AWS CDK with multi-environment context variables
quiz.explanation: StackSets are designed exactly for deploying and managing stacks across multiple AWS accounts and regions from a single operation, and they integrate with AWS Organizations for organization-wide deployments. Nested Stacks manage template composition within a single stack, not multi-account deployments. Change Sets deployed sequentially would require enormous manual effort across 250 combinations. CDK generates CloudFormation but still needs StackSets for multi-account deployment.

quiz.question: A team is building a serverless application with AWS Lambda functions and API Gateway. They want to define the infrastructure using familiar Lambda-specific shorthand syntax that automatically transforms into CloudFormation. Which tool should they use?
options: AWS CDK with TypeScript constructs
AWS SAM (Serverless Application Model)
AWS Elastic Beanstalk
AWS CodeDeploy with Lambda deployment configuration
quiz.explanation: AWS SAM extends CloudFormation with shorthand syntax (e.g., AWS::Serverless::Function) that transforms into standard CloudFormation resources, making it ideal for serverless applications with Lambda and API Gateway. CDK uses general-purpose programming languages and is not Lambda-specific shorthand. Elastic Beanstalk is a PaaS for web applications. CodeDeploy deploys code to existing Lambda functions but doesn't define infrastructure.

quiz.question: A company has an existing on-premises data center with Linux servers and wants to use AWS CodeDeploy to automate application deployments to those servers. Which statement is accurate about this configuration?
options: CodeDeploy cannot target on-premises servers; it only supports EC2 instances
CodeDeploy supports on-premises servers by installing the CodeDeploy agent on them
CodeDeploy will first provision new EC2 instances and then deploy the application
CodeDeploy requires AWS OpsWorks to manage the on-premises servers before deployment
quiz.explanation: CodeDeploy supports EC2, ECS, Lambda, AND on-premises servers. By installing the CodeDeploy agent on on-premises servers and registering them as instances, CodeDeploy can deploy application code to them. CodeDeploy never provisions new resources — it deploys only to EXISTING compute resources. OpsWorks is a separate configuration management service, not a prerequisite for CodeDeploy.

quiz.question: A company wants to run containerized workloads with the LEAST operational overhead. The team has no preference between Docker and Kubernetes. Which TWO deployment options allow them to run containers without managing EC2 instances? (Choose TWO)
options: ECS with EC2 launch type
ECS with Fargate launch type
EKS with self-managed node groups
EKS with Fargate
ECS Anywhere on on-premises hardware
quiz.explanation: Both ECS Fargate and EKS Fargate are serverless container options where AWS manages the underlying compute infrastructure — no EC2 instances to provision or patch. ECS EC2 and EKS with self-managed node groups require managing EC2 instances. ECS Anywhere runs on on-premises or other infrastructure, adding operational burden.

quiz.question: An organization uses AWS CloudFormation to manage infrastructure. After a manual change was made directly in the AWS Console to a CloudFormation-managed security group, the team wants to identify what has changed. Which CloudFormation capability addresses this?
options: Change Set — to preview what a new template update would change
Drift Detection — to identify differences between expected and actual resource state
Stack Policy — to prevent unauthorized changes to stack resources
Rollback triggers — to revert the stack to a previous state automatically
quiz.explanation: Drift Detection identifies resources whose actual configuration has deviated from the expected CloudFormation template state — exactly what's needed after a manual Console change. Change Sets preview planned template updates, not existing manual changes. Stack Policies prevent certain updates but don't detect what has already changed. Rollback triggers monitor deployments in progress, not post-deployment manual changes.

quiz.question: A company runs a monolithic application that needs configuration management using a well-known DSL (domain-specific language) framework. Their operations team has deep expertise in Chef. Which AWS service provides a managed Chef environment with the LEAST migration overhead?
options: AWS CodeDeploy with lifecycle hooks for configuration
AWS CloudFormation with cfn-init metadata
AWS OpsWorks for Chef Automate
AWS Elastic Beanstalk with .ebextensions configuration files
quiz.explanation: AWS OpsWorks provides managed Chef Automate and Puppet Enterprise environments, allowing teams with existing Chef expertise to use their recipes directly with minimal migration overhead.
